page: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 64 physical cores with a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong overall performance, particularly in memory and compute-intensive tasks. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Passmark memory tests. Multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth consistently rank in the top 25 percent of tested hardware. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also achieves top-tier performance in LLM text generation using medium-sized 7B models.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a potential performance tradeoff under sustained, highly concurrent workloads compared to dedicated CPU instances. However, the high resource density of 64 cores and 192.0 GB of RAM offers qualitative cost efficiency for parallel processing tasks that do not require constant, dedicated CPU execution. This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 64 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor, 192.0 GB of RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance alongside strong multi-core capabilities. Benchmark data highlights its efficiency in memory bandwidth, Redis operations, and medium-scale LLM text generation. The processor features a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention under sustained peak loads, the high core density provides qualitative cost efficiency for parallel workloads. This server is suited for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 64 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.

tagline: A high-density AMD EPYC compute instance featuring sixty-four shared cores, large memory bandwidth, and local NVMe SSD storage.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core
960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
